comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: "`--pr "#123"` → Written Status: `done`"
Reality: The `update_node_in_frontmatter()` function infers `in_progress` when a PR is set (not `done`). The code at `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py:340` shows: `new_status = cast(RoadmapNodeStatus, "in_progress")` — this is the elif branch triggered when `resolved_pr` is truthy. Setting `--status done` explicitly is required to get `done` status.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py:338-340`

Suggested fix: Change the table row to show `in_progress` for `--pr "#123"` without explicit status. The `done` status only occurs when `--status done` is passed explicitly. This also contradicts `roadmap-status-system.md` line 47 which correctly says PR inference → `in_progress`.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Fixed in 7ab7c465a: Updated inference rule description from done to in_progress to match update_node_in_frontmatter() source.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-25 03:24 UTC_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: "2. **PR is set** → status = `"done"`"
Reality: The source code at `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py:340` explicitly sets `new_status = cast(RoadmapNodeStatus, "in_progress")` when a PR is set without an explicit status. The inferred status is `in_progress`, not `done`.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py:338-342`

Suggested fix: Change to: "2. **PR is set** → status = `"in_progress"`"

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Fixed in 7ab7c465a: Changed "done" to "in_progress" to match source code at roadmap.py:340.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-25 03:24 UTC_

comments.body:

Claim: "update-objective-node `--pr "#123"` → Result Status: `done`"
Reality: Per `update_node_in_frontmatter()` in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py:340`, setting `--pr "#123"` without explicit `--status` infers `in_progress`, not `done`.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py:338-340`

Suggested fix: Change `done` to `in_progress` in this row. This table directly contradicts `roadmap-status-system.md` line 47 which correctly documents the inference as `in_progress`.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: Table row showing `--status in_progress` (without `--pr`) as a valid usage pattern.
Reality: The `--pr` flag is `required=True` in the Click command at `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/update_objective_node.py:195`. The command will fail with a missing option error if `--pr` is omitted. This table row implies `--status` can be used standalone, which is incorrect.

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/update_objective_node.py:192-197`

Suggested fix: Remove the `--status in_progress` standalone row, or note that `--pr` is always required (even if set to `'''`).

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Fixed: replaced standalone `--status in_progress` row with `--pr "#123" --status in_progress` since --pr is required=True

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-24 21:03 PST_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — private function name in prose.

Claim: "The update command uses `_replace_node_refs_in_body()` to update the YAML frontmatter"
Issue: Per learned-docs-core.md content rules: "Private `_underscore` methods must never appear — they are the most volatile identifiers in a codebase and their behavior belongs in docstrings, not learned docs."

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/update_objective_node.py, _replace_node_refs_in_body`

Suggested fix: Remove the private function name from prose. Replace with: "The update command updates the YAML frontmatter (source of truth), then re-renders the comment table via `rerender_comment_roadmap()`" — `rerender_comment_roadmap` is a public function and appropriate to name.

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Fixed in b7d83ffbc — removed private `_replace_node_refs_in_body()` from prose, now describes the operation and references public `rerender_comment_roadmap()`.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-24 21:13 PST_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: `--pr ""` → Written Status: `pending`
Reality: When `pr=""` is passed, `update_node_in_frontmatter()` resolves `resolved_pr = None` (clears it), then falls to `else: new_status = step.status` — preserving the **existing** status, not forcing `pending`. A node with `status: done` that has its PR cleared would remain `done`.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py, update_node_in_frontmatter` (lines 335-342)

Suggested fix: Change table row to: `--pr ""` → `(preserved)` | `(cleared)`. If the intent is to set `pending` when clearing PR, add `elif pr == "" and resolved_pr is None: new_status = "pending"` to the gateway code and update the test `test_clear_pr_reference` accordingly.

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Fixed in 082f15fbc — changed `pending` to `(preserved)` in the status table to match actual code behavior (line 342 of roadmap.py: `new_status = step.status`).

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-25 01:39 PST_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body: **Tripwires Review**: Tripwire removed from frontmatter without running `erk docs sync` - The PR removes a tripwire from the `tripwires:` section in frontmatter (the "setting PR reference without providing --plan" tripwire), but did not regenerate the category tripwire files. According to docs/learned/documentation/frontmatter-tripwire-format.md: "Any change to title, read_when, or tripwires fields in frontmatter affects generated files. The sync pipeline must run before committing." Please run `erk docs sync` to regenerate docs/learned/architecture/tripwires.md and docs/learned/tripwires-index.md.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Verified: erk docs sync was already run in commit dde73fc91. Re-ran to confirm — all files up to date.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-25 03:24 UTC_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: `--pr ""` → Written Status: `pending` / "Clear PR reference resets status"
Reality: Per `update_node_in_frontmatter()` in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py` (lines 335-342): when PR is cleared (`resolved_pr = None`), the status inference falls to `else: new_status = step.status` — **preserving existing status**, not forcing `pending`.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py, update_node_in_frontmatter`

Same issue also appears at lines 72-73, 140. Suggested fix: Update all three table rows and the comment `# Clear PR reference (resets to pending)` to reflect that status is preserved, not reset to pending. If `pending` is the intended behavior, the gateway code should be updated to force it.

comments.body:
Fixed in 082f15fbc — updated all three locations (lines 39, 72-73, 140) to show `(preserved)` instead of `pending` and corrected the comment from "resets to pending" to "preserves existing status".

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-25 01:39 PST_

comments.body:

Claim: `# Single step -- set PR reference (marks done)`
Reality: Setting `--pr <ref>` without `--status done` marks the step `in_progress`, not done. The status computation table immediately above (lines 36-39) correctly shows this: `--pr #123` → `in-progress`. Only `--pr #123 --status done` produces `done`.

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/update_objective_node.py`

Suggested fix: Change the comment to `# Single step -- link PR reference (marks in_progress)` or `# Single step -- set PR reference`.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Fixed in f5b907f: Changed comment to "marks in_progress" since --pr without --status done marks the step in_progress, not done.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claims detected in this document (pre-existing issues surfaced by full-doc audit on touch).

Multiple sections describe a `plan` field on roadmap nodes that no longer exists in the PR-only model:
- Lines 118, 123, 128: YAML examples include `plan: null`
- Line 200 (Surgical Update flow): "Update `plan`/`pr` fields"
- Lines 211, 213: Usage examples with `--pr "plan #6464"` and status inference from `plan #`
- Line 250: `plan-save.md Step 3.5` still uses `--pr "plan #<plan-number>"`
- Line 266: Frontmatter schema includes `plan: null # Optional...`
- Line 362: Flow diagram shows `set pr="plan #N"`

Reality: `RoadmapNode` no longer has a `plan` field (removed in this PR). The schema is now 4-column (Node/Description/Status/PR).

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py, RoadmapNode`

Suggested fix: Remove all `plan:` field references from YAML examples, update the surgical update flow to remove step 4's "plan" mention, update usage examples and flow diagram.

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Fixed in 082f15fbc — removed all stale `plan:` field references: YAML examples (lines 118/123/128), mutation flow step 4, usage examples (replaced `plan #` with `--status planning`), frontmatter schema, and data flow diagram.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-25 01:40 PST_

Claim: "2. Updates the `_replace_node_refs_in_body()` regex pattern in the YAML block"
Reality: This step description is garbled. `_replace_node_refs_in_body()` is not a "regex pattern in the YAML block" — it is the function called to update the YAML frontmatter (which internally calls `update_node_in_frontmatter()`). Step 1 already covers the YAML update. This step reads like an editing artifact.

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/update_objective_node.py:130-186`

Suggested fix: Remove step 2 entirely, or replace with something accurate like "2. Serializes updated YAML back into the metadata block and replaces it in the issue body".

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Tripwires Review**: Private function `_replace_table_in_text` referenced in prose

Per docs/learned/architecture/roadmap-mutation-semantics.md tripwire (line 239 of docs/learned/architecture/tripwires.md): 'Private `_underscore` methods must never appear in learned docs — they are the most volatile identifiers in a codebase.'

This line reads: 'This replaced the previous approach of per-node regex patching (`_replace_table_in_text`), which was fragile...'

Suggested fix: Remove the function name entirely. Replace with: 'This replaced the previous approach of per-node regex patching, which was fragile and couldn't handle column layout changes.'

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — private function name in source pointer.

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/update_objective_node.py, _replace_node_refs_in_body`

Per learned-docs-core.md: "Private `_underscore` methods must never appear — they are the most volatile identifiers in a codebase and their behavior belongs in docstrings, not learned docs."

The source pointer at this line names the private function `_replace_node_refs_in_body`. While the function still exists, private functions are the most volatile identifiers and should never appear in docs.

Suggested fix: Replace with a name-based pointer to the public function or module level:
```markdown
<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/update_objective_node.py, update_objective_node -->
```

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Fixed — replaced private `_replace_node_refs_in_body` with public `update_objective_node` in source pointer.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-24 21:31 PST_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Tripwires Review**: Private function `_replace_node_refs_in_body` referenced in test docstring

Per docs/learned/architecture/roadmap-mutation-semantics.md guidance: 'Private `_underscore` methods must never appear in learned docs — they are the most volatile identifiers in a codebase.'

While test code has more flexibility than learned docs, test docstrings should describe behavior, not implementation details. This docstring reads: '`_replace_node_refs_in_body` with new_pr=None preserves existing PR.'

Suggested fix: Replace with behavior-focused description: 'Passing new_pr=None to the update function preserves existing PR reference.' This remains accurate even if the internal function is renamed or refactored.

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Fixed in c5135d0c0 — replaced private function name `_replace_node_refs_in_body` with behavior-focused description: "Passing new_pr=None to the update function preserves existing PR reference."

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-25 02:24 PST_
